⚙ Advanced Settings
▼
📻 Radio Ready 🔥 Viral 🎨 Artistic 🧪 Experimental
🎷 50s 1950–1959 ✌️ 60s 1960–1969 🕺 70s 1970–1979 📼 80s–90s 1980–1999 💿 2000s 2000–2015 📱 2010s 2016–2022 🔥 Now 2023–Now
None 🎷 Classic 📼 80s–90s 💿 2000s 📱 2010s 🔥 Now
onto
🌊 Mellow ◼ Mid 🔥 Heat 💢 Rage
No Brackets Basic Enhanced Suno Optimized ✦ Experimental
Standard Adventurous Avant-garde
Diatonic, genre-typical progressions — the proven formulas that work
Auto (genre default) Straight — tight grid Shuffle — loose & warm Heavy Swing — J Dilla drag
Matched to genre automatically — affects both production and lyric rhythm
Auto (from swing) Dense — pack every beat Normal — balanced Sparse — space is music
Syllable count follows swing feel by default — override here for specific density
Southpaw Rhyme Scheme
Plant rhymes early, pay off late — deferred tension. Works in every genre.
Syllable Hard Cap
Max syllables per line — enforced on every section. 0 = no cap (genre default).
Suno Udio Stable Audio
Output optimized for Suno — bracket tags, under-200-char prompts
No emotional arc override — the song follows the topic's natural emotional direction
Dual Perspective (Verse 2)
Verse 2 written from the antagonist or opposing POV — creates dramatic tension.
Genre-appropriate scale selected automatically
Auto (from genre) Smooth Sung Melodic / Auto-Tune Chanted Call & Response Screamed / Crunk Southern Drawl Rapped Hook Minimal / Groove Mantra Double Hook A+B Gospel Vamp Post-Hook Drop

📋 Paste Lyrics
🎭 Alternate Take

Reimagine this song through a completely different stylistic lens. Same topic and emotional arc — different production, flow, ad-libs, and regional vocabulary.

Pick a pairing below. Examples: a Southern rapper on a G-Funk beat, a New York lyricist on Dr. Dre production, or a cross-genre hybrid like Linkin Park's rap-rock formula.

Tip: Alt Takes use your current song as source material — the original stays intact. Each generation is a separate AI call.

Vocable DNA — cross-genre map ▸
Sound Genres Function
yeahHip-hop · R&B · Country · Rock · Pop · GospelAd-lib → affirmation → anthem → congregation
oohR&B · Pop · Gospel · Soul · K-pop · BluesMelisma → crowd hook → spiritual overwhelm
heyAfrobeats · Pop · Punk · Country · RockCall-response → section opener → collective fury
na-na-naPop · Punk · Folk · ReggaeSingalong placeholder → nihilist filler → mantra
ah / ohGospel · Soul · R&B · Blues · CountrySpiritual overflow → emotional peak → field holler
uh / ayyHip-hop · Trap · Reggaeton · AfrobeatsRhythmic filler → dembow accent → groove punctuation
Lineage: Gospel → Soul → R&B → Pop → Hip-hop → Afrobeats  ·  Folk → Country → Pop  ·  Blues → Rock → Punk

🚀 Campaign Mode
Generate a Full Song Campaign
Create multiple song concepts at once — each with unique lyrics, a hook, production brief, and Suno/Udio prompt. Perfect for artists, producers, and content creators building a release campaign.
🎼 Album Engine
Album-Level Creative Planning
Generate a complete album concept — emotional arc, track map, sonic palette, commercial strategy, and gap analysis. Turn an idea into a release plan.
